meta.title: How I Made an AI Short Film (Seedream 4.5 + Kling 3.0)
meta.description:
In this tutorial, I'll show you how I made the cinematic AI short film "Calamity"  using Seedream 4.5 for image generation and Kling 3.0 Omni for video, all inside the new OpenArt Suite. You'll see the full workflow: casting a character, building consistent locations, creating action scenes (a falling piano and a car crash through a cafe wall), and keeping everything visually consistent across shots. Stay for the full film at the end or check out the chapters to jump ahead. (Fun fact: the character Cleo is named after Agnes Varda's New Wave classic "Cleo from 5 to 7" about a woman waiting for test results.)

🔗 OpenArt https://openart.ai/home/?via=aivideoschool
(I made "Calamity" using credits in the Infinite plan)
Affiliate links support this channel!

✍️ Prompts - get all the prompts I used in this movie 
https://aivideoschool.xyz/tutorials/prompts-for-ai-short-film-calamity-seedream-kling/

🎬 AI Video School
https://aivideoschool.xyz/
My AI filmmaking course launches early March!

📬 Sign up for my newsletter and get my free AI Video Prompt Sidekick for ChatGPT
https://aivideoschool.substack.com/

📚Tutorial Chapters
00:00 Preview of "Calamity" and intro
00:29 Casting your AI movie
02:49 Creating consistent scenes
05:40 Piano falling scene (first calamity)
06:27 Cafe car crash scene (second calamity)
07:41 Consistent location/set (the real Calamity!)
09:11 Watch the final movie! "Calamity" starring Cleo (and her cat)

#AIFilmmaking #Seedream #Kling3 #AIShortFilm #OpenArt #openartai #aivideotutorial #aitutorialforbeginners #aicinema #cinematicai

and almost hits her. So, there's a

Right. For this car crash scene, I did

Then when the car crash happens, I

inside the car with the glass all over

Weirdly enough, Clling mixed up who is

For her apartment scene, I wanted to

but I get different angles of the room.
